one_line_takeaway: DF-LoGiT shows that a purely analytic, training-free weight edit exploiting ViT's multi-head attention and [CLS] residual stream can implant a near-100% successful, m-of-n logic-gated backdoor into a released checkpoint in milliseconds, evading Neural Cleanse, Fine-Pruning, and Patch Processing, and only being partially mitigated (not defeated) by the ViT-specific BDVT defense.
key_finding: 1-of-1 attack (Table 1): DeiT-Tiny baseline C-ACC 72.13% -> edited 70.93% (Delta -1.20%), ASR 99.85%, injection time 3.62 ms; DeiT-Small baseline C-ACC 79.83% -> edited 79.36% (Delta -0.47%), ASR 100.00%, injection time 3.26 ms; ViT-B baseline C-ACC 80.99% -> edited 79.67% (Delta -1.32%), ASR 100.00%, injection time 2.70 ms. 2-of-3 attack on DeiT-Small: aggregated benign-mode C-ACC averaged 78.23% (Delta -1.60%, average TLLR 0.24%, one-trigger TLLR average 0.26%); individual two-trigger-combo ASRs were right+left 99.79%, right+top 95.10%, left+top 100.00%, and all-three-triggers ASR 100.00%, with aggregated ASR over |S|>=2 at 98.72%; one-shot injection time for the 2-of-3 attack was 43.55 ms. The DFBA baseline directly ported to ViTs failed: clean-utility collapse (C-ACC=2.12%) and ASR=0.00%, attributed to global self-attention token mixing dispersing the CNN-style localized pathway evidence (near-chance [CLS] AUC ~0.5) versus DF-LoGiT's near-1.0 AUC separability. Defense evaluation (Table 2, 2-of-3 protocol): Neural Cleanse failed to recover the true target label (found a wrong "Pencil sharpener" label vs. real "Goldfish") and its inverted trigger was ineffective (ASR 0.03%); Fine-Pruning left ASR2-avg 92.51% / ASR3 100% after pruning+fine-tuning (C-ACC 79.55%, Delta +0.67%); Patch Processing left ASR2-avg 93.14% / ASR3 100% (C-ACC 76.07%, Delta -2.81%); BDVT substantially reduced two-trigger ASR to 14.38% but failed against the fully redundant three-trigger case (ASR3 98.82%, C-ACC 78.58%, Delta -0.30%). The paper also reports an empirical probability Pr[Delta_gate(alpha,beta) >= 3.31] = 0.999 for the theoretical evidence margin.
core_contribution: DF-LoGiT is presented as the first truly data-free, training-free, architecture-preserving backdoor attack on Vision Transformers (ViTs), implanted purely via direct analytic weight editing of a released checkpoint. It (1) constructs closed-form trigger patches by back-projecting from a chosen key-projection coordinate in a Block-0 attention head so the stamped patch produces a large, controllable key activation; (2) amplifies the corresponding Q/K coordinate by gain alpha and rewrites the value projection (WV) to align with that coordinate, then rewrites a column of the output projection (WO, gain beta) to route the resulting evidence into a reserved [CLS] embedding coordinate g; (3) preserves that [CLS] state exactly across intermediate blocks by rewriting the attention/MLP write-back rows/columns for coordinate g to near-zero norm, forcing the residual shortcut to be the only transport path; (4) performs a conditional payload injection only in the final block, via a single-neuron GELU gate reading coordinate g that shifts the [CLS] representation along the normalized target-class classifier-weight direction (v_dir) only when the gate fires; and (5) generalizes to an m-of-n Boolean co-occurrence trigger by replicating the mechanism across n head/location pairs and aggregating per-trigger indicator bits with a single hidden neuron in Block 0's MLP. The paper provides two theoretical guarantees (Lemma 1: a constant margin Delta_gate(alpha,beta) separates trigger vs. benign evidence at the written [CLS] coordinate; and an exact-preservation result showing zero intermediate write-back implies x_CLS[g] is unchanged block-to-block) and validates both mechanistically (AUC separability, gate pre-activation separation, depth-wise signal-preservation traces).
threat_model: Supply-chain attacker with white-box access to a publicly released, pre-trained ViT checkpoint (e.g., from a model hub), who can modify and republish the checkpoint but has NO access to training, validation, surrogate, or synthetic data, performs NO optimization or fine-tuning, and makes NO architectural changes to the model or inference pipeline. The only artifact of the attack is the republished checkpoint itself; the backdoor is injected via a millisecond-scale analytic weight rewrite. Attacker goal: inputs satisfying an attacker-chosen trigger protocol (single trigger, or m-of-n co-occurrence of patch triggers) are mapped to an attacker-chosen target label, while benign inputs (including sub-threshold trigger counts) remain largely unaffected and the backdoor should evade deployment-time defenses (the paper explicitly excludes training-phase defenses as incompatible with this threat model).
limitations: The paper's own scope restricts the threat model to strict data-free checkpoint rewriting; it does not claim to address attackers who can retrain, fine-tune, or access any data. Evaluation is limited to image classification (ImageNet-1K) on three specific backbones (DeiT-Tiny, DeiT-Small, ViT-B) with 16x16 patch-aligned triggers only (Fig. 4); generalization to other trigger geometries, other vision-transformer families, or other tasks (e.g., detection, segmentation, multimodal ViTs) is not evaluated. BDVT, one of the two ViT-specific defenses, does meaningfully suppress the two-trigger attack mode (ASR2-avg down to 14.38%), showing the m-of-n design is not uniformly robust across all attack-mode compositions -- full robustness only holds when trigger redundancy (all three triggers present) compensates. The paper does not evaluate against defenses that specifically search for logic-gated or co-occurrence triggers, nor against provenance/integrity-based supply-chain defenses it recommends in its own Impact Statement. Only one baseline (DFBA, a CNN-native method) is compared against; no other ViT-specific data-free or optimization-based backdoor baseline (e.g., Lv et al. DBIA) is empirically compared head-to-head.

quantitative_results: 1-of-1 DeiT-Tiny: C-ACC 72.13% -> 70.93% (Delta -1.20%), ASR 99.85%, injection time 3.62 ms
1-of-1 DeiT-Small: C-ACC 79.83% -> 79.36% (Delta -0.47%), ASR 100.00%, injection time 3.26 ms
1-of-1 ViT-B: C-ACC 80.99% -> 79.67% (Delta -1.32%), ASR 100.00%, injection time 2.70 ms

2-of-3 DeiT-Small: benign-mode avg C-ACC 78.23% (Delta -1.60%), avg TLLR 0.24%; ASR right+left 99.79%, right+top 95.10%, left+top 100.00%, all-three 100.00%; aggregated ASR (|S|>=2) 98.72%; injection time 43.55 ms
DFBA-on-ViT baseline: C-ACC collapses to 2.12%, ASR 0.00%, [CLS] AUC ~0.5 (near chance)

Defense results (2-of-3): Neural Cleanse inverted-trigger ASR 0.03% (wrong label recovered: Pencil sharpener vs true Goldfish); Fine-Pruning C-ACC 79.55% (Delta +0.67%), ASR2-avg 92.51%, ASR3 100%; Patch Processing C-ACC 76.07% (Delta -2.81%), ASR2-avg 93.14%, ASR3 100%; BDVT C-ACC 78.58% (Delta -0.30%), ASR2-avg 14.38%, ASR3 98.82%
baselines_compared: DFBA (Cao et al., 2024) -- CNN data-free backdoor attack, direct-transfer baseline to ViTs
No-defense baseline (2-of-3 setting): C-ACC 78.88%, ASR2-avg 98.30%, ASR3 100.00%
